text: The surgeon performs a transanal submucosal dissection of a large lower rectal adenoma using the Thunderbit tissue management system. The adenoma, approximately four centimeters in size, is located from the dentin line to the lower rectum and has a soft consistency.

The surgeon performs submucosal dissection of the lower rectal adenoma using a traditional trans-anal approach with Thunderbit assistance. The anal retractor provides a clear view of the tumor margin.

The surgeon injects normal saline mixed with two percent lidocaine to elevate the submucosa around the tumor. A scalpel is used to mark the lowest dissection margin from the dentin line, followed by the use of the Thunderbit device for submucosal dissection.

The surgeon identifies the dissection plane and performs hemostasis using the Thunderbit device. The anal sphincter muscle is visible, and the dissection extends up to the lower rectum, with a clear view of the dissection plan under the transanal approach.
The surgeon ligates the pedicle of the tumor with a clamp and then transects it using the Thunderbit device. The stump is subsequently suture ligated with 3-0 Vicryl.
The whole procedure was performed smoothly within thirty minutes with minimal blood loss. The patient was discharged one day after the surgery.
The final pathology proved cecil serrated adenoma with free surgical margin.
